mnemonics.title: Template Syntax Reminder
mnemonics.content: TEMPLATE = T(ype) + EMPLATE - Think 'T' for Type parameter, 'EMPLATE' sounds like 'complete' - complete type handling

mnemonics.title: typename vs class
mnemonics.content: typename = TYPE NAME - both keywords create type parameters; 'typename' emphasizes it's a name representing a type

mnemonics.title: Template Instantiation Memory
mnemonics.content: STACK<int> s; - Think '<' means 'less than' but actually 'angle brackets contain the TYPE'

acronyms.usage: Provides ready-to-use template classes like vector, list, stack, map

acronyms.expansion: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
acronyms.usage: Template technique where invalid substitutions cause overload failure rather than errors
Base class template that takes derived class as parameter for compile-time polymorphism
Name lookup rule for function templates that finds functions in namespace of template arguments

visualTips.visualization: Draw template<class T> as a 'header' above the class box, with T floating above as a placeholder that gets filled when instantiated
Show template class as a 'form' and instantiated class as 'filled form' - the template is the mold, instantiation creates the object
Draw a main template tree branch with 'primary template' at root, then 'specialization branches' for specific types like int*, char*
Show template<int SIZE> as having a 'constant box' next to type box - size is fixed at compile time like an integer constant

analogies.analogy: Like a cookie cutter (template) making cookies (instantiations) - same shape, different ingredients (types)
T is like a blank space on a form - gets filled when someone fills out the form (instantiates the class)
Like a general recipe book (generic template) plus special dietary versions (specializations) for specific needs
Like a translation done before delivery - templates are resolved at compile time, unlike runtime polymorphism which is like on-demand translation
